name: Roasted Shallot-Potato Cake '21' Club
recipeIngredient: Freshly ground black pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the olive oil in a skillet over moderately high heat until hot but not smoking.
2. Saute the shallots, stirring occasionally, until soft and caramelized, about 10 to 15 minutes.
3. Peel the potatoes and shred them using a food processor with a fine julienne or shredding blade, or grate them coarsely on small teardrop-shaped holes of a 4-sided grater.
4. Stir the shredded thyme, pepper, and salt into the sauteed shallots.
5. Preheat the oven to 325 degrees Fahrenheit.
6. In an ovenproof 10-inch non-stick skillet (or a 10-inch non-stick skillet with handle covered with a double thickness of foil), heat butter over moderately high heat until hot but not smoking.
7. Spread half of the shredded potatoes evenly over the bottom of the skillet, pressing gently.
8. Spread the sauteed shallots over the potatoes using a slotted spoon and top with the remaining shredded potatoes in an even layer, gently pressing with the back of a spatula.
9. Cook the potato cake over high heat until the underside begins to brown, about 2 minutes.
10. Reduce the heat to low and cook until the underside is golden, about 5 to 7 minutes.
11. Roast the potato cake in the oven until the underside is a rich golden brown, about 15 minutes (the top will remain white).
12. Carefully invert the potato cake onto a plate or baking sheet without sides and slide it back into the skillet.
13. Continue roasting for an additional 20 minutes.
14. Sprinkle with additional pepper and salt to taste and cut into wedges.
Makes: 4 to 6 servings.
